one_liner: Adobe Commerce (Magento) offers broad REST/GraphQL/SOAP APIs for ecommerce operations, but self-serve access requires admin-level integration setup on an existing (self-hosted or licensed) instance rather than instant signup, with auth varying by deployment type.

auth_methods.notes: Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service (SaaS) requires Adobe IMS OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens for all REST API requests.
PaaS/on-premise installations use OAuth 1.0a for third-party apps.
Mobile/automated apps use token-based authentication (admin or customer login token).
Admin/customer login credentials used to obtain access tokens.

self_serve_notes: No free multi-tenant SaaS signup with self-serve API keys; requires an existing Adobe Commerce/Magento instance (self-hosted Open Source or licensed Adobe Commerce/Cloud) and admin panel access to create integrations/tokens. Magento Open Source is free and self-hostable, giving developers full self-serve control once installed, but there's no hosted free tier or instant API key issuance from Adobe.

api_surface.notes: Supports REST, GraphQL, and SOAP APIs across catalog, orders, customers, CMS, integrations. Endpoint set varies between PaaS (Adobe Commerce/Magento Open Source) and SaaS (Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service, GraphQL-focused with reduced REST surface).

main_blocker: No unified, official self-serve credential flow; auth method and API surface (REST vs GraphQL, endpoint availability) vary significantly depending on deployment type (Open Source, PaaS Adobe Commerce, or SaaS Cloud Service), requiring per-instance configuration and admin-level integration setup rather than a simple developer signup.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API auth methods (OAuth 1.0a, token, login credentials) and differences between PaaS and SaaS endpoint sets.
Confirms REST/GraphQL/SOAP support, three auth types, and requirement to register a web service/integration via Admin panel (admin-approval self-serve model).
Details Adobe IMS OAuth2 requirement for Cloud Service REST API authentication.
Confirms GraphQL API availability and endpoint structure differences between PaaS and SaaS.
Notes no free hosted tier for Adobe Commerce; free self-hosted Magento Open Source alternative exists with API limitations.

flags: Auth method and API surface differ substantially between Magento Open Source, Adobe Commerce PaaS, and Adobe Commerce SaaS - toolkit must handle multiple auth/endpoint variants
No official Adobe-hosted MCP server; existing MCP implementations are third-party/community (e.g., thomastx05/magento-mcp, rafaelcg/adobe-commerce-dev-mcp, mcp-hub.ink)
No free instant sandbox/dev account; requires an actual store instance (self-hosted or licensed) to obtain credentials
Webhooks not natively supported per docs, which may limit event-driven agent use cases
